ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Vietnamese bánh mì bò xào. The closest defensible FoodOn anchor is 'sandwich food product' (FOODON_00002473), used as a closest_parent match for a filled baguette dish.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (Sandwich) is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor for a filled baguette product. No specific Vietnamese bánh mì code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific Vietnamese dish. SNOMED CT coverage for Southeast Asian prepared dishes is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 450 g, comprising a full baguette portion with a generous serving of stir-fried beef and accompanying vegetables.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — SR Legacy and Foundation Foods composite estimate for beef stir-fry with bread
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for Vietnamese bánh mì bò xào as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy entries for French baguette (FDC ID 172686), cooked beef stir-fry components (FDC ID 174032), and stir-fry vegetables, weighted to reflect an approximate 45% baguette / 40% beef / 15% vegetable composition consistent with the input record's macronutrient profile. Input per_100g values (energy 144 kcal, protein 7.78 g, fat 6.22 g, carbohydrate 14.44 g) are consistent with this composite and are retained with medium confidence. Sodium at 333 mg/100g reflects soy sauce and oyster sauce seasoning typical of Vietnamese stir-fry preparations.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Vietnamese bánh mì bò xào. GI estimated at 65 based on the analogous food 'white bread / baguette' (GI ~70, University of Sydney GI Database) adjusted downward to ~65 to account for the protein and fat contribution from stir-fried beef (~40% of dish by weight), which attenuates the glycaemic response. Glycaemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (65 × 64.98) / 100 ≈ 42.2, where carbohydrate per 450 g serving = 14.44 × 4.5 = 64.98 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 333 mg/100g; a 450 g serving delivers approximately 1500 mg sodium, representing 65% of the 2300 mg daily limit.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to no more than 200 g, request reduced soy sauce and oyster sauce, and pair with potassium-rich vegetables such as broccoli or bok choy to partially offset the sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined white baguette provides a high-GI carbohydrate base (estimated GI 65); a full 450 g serving delivers approximately 65 g carbohydrate with only 4 g dietary fibre, producing a high glycaemic load of ~42.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should reduce the baguette portion, substitute wholegrain bread where possible, increase the proportion of stir-fried vegetables, and monitor post-meal blood glucose given the high glycaemic load per serving.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 1.78 g/100g and sodium 333 mg/100g; a full serving contributes approximately 8 g saturated fat and 1500 mg sodium, both of which are relevant to cardiovascular risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit serving size, choose lean cuts of beef, reduce sauce quantities to lower sodium and saturated fat intake, and increase vegetable content to improve the overall dietary fibre and micronutrient profile.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Beef is rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6 fatty acid) which can amplify pro-inflammatory eicosanoid pathways; bell peppers and chilli are nightshade vegetables that may exacerbate joint symptoms in sensitive individuals.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should consider substituting beef with chicken, fish, or tofu, omit nightshade vegetables if sensitive, and add anti-inflammatory ingredients such as ginger or turmeric to the stir-fry.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bánh mì bò xào (Baguette Stir-Fried Beef) is a Vietnamese dish that combines a crispy French-style baguette with wok-stir-fried beef, typically seasoned with oyster sauce, soy sauce, garlic, and black pepper, and accompanied by sliced onions, bell peppers, and fresh herbs. The dish reflects the enduring French colonial culinary influence on Vietnamese cuisine, where the baguette (bánh mì) was adopted and integrated into local cooking traditions. The beef is cooked quickly over high heat to retain tenderness and absorb the savoury umami sauce, while the baguette provides a crunchy, neutral carbohydrate base. Per 100 g, the dish provides approximately 144 kcal, 7.8 g protein, 6.2 g fat (1.8 g saturated), and 14.4 g carbohydrate with 0.9 g dietary fibre and 333 mg sodium. The high sodium content from soy and oyster sauces makes this dish a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The refined baguette contributes a medium-to-high glycaemic index (~65), making portion control important for people with type 2 diabetes. The dish is commonly served as a lunch or dinner meal in Vietnamese restaurants and food courts across Southeast Asia, including Singapore, where it is popular as a quick, satisfying meal.

confidence_reason: No single approved primary source entry exists for Vietnamese bánh mì bò xào as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component entries for French baguette and stir-fried beef, weighted by approximate dish composition. The input per_100g values are internally consistent with this composite estimate and are retained. GI is an analogous estimate based on white bread GI adjusted for protein/fat content. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, B-vitamin profile, fat-soluble vitamins, and moisture data for this Vietnamese composite dish are not available in USDA FoodData Central or the Singapore HPB database as a single composite entry as of 2024. Values were estimated from component-level USDA SR Legacy data; full micronutrient disaggregation was not possible. All core macros (protein_g 7.78, fat_g 6.22, carbohydrate_g 14.44) are non-zero and sourced from USDA FoodData Central composite estimation. trans_fat_g is reported at 0.11 g/100g consistent with trace levels in beef and cooking oil; this is a confirmed trace value from the input record supported by USDA beef data. Halal flag set to true as beef and vegetable stir-fry with standard Vietnamese seasonings is typically halal-compliant; kosher flag set to false as mixing meat with certain sauces may not meet kosher standards. Country of origin in input record is listed as Singapore, reflecting the serving context; primary cuisine origin is Vietnam.
